llm_context.content: Imagine you are helping software test engineers to create comprehensive test plans without delving i...

actionUnits.condition: Scene config confirms MonoBehaviour_0 uses script guid 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d (Fire.cs). Initial serialized values: isOn=0, shouldTurnOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxHitInterval=2, totalHitTime=5, mainEffectGo set, BoxCollider present, no Rigidbody. This step prepares the object for testing; no direct method invocations. Note: Awake() will randomize timeToOn in [40..60].

Simulate two neighbors turning ON to satisfy threshold: call Fire.NotifyNeighborState(true) twice on this component (script component fileID likely 2592690533154406537 from prefab; GUID 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d). This should set onNeighbors >= onNeighborsThreshold and cause CheckNeighborState() to set shouldTurnOn=true and reset timeToOnDelta=0. Note: neighbors list may be empty; that's acceptable—hasDownNeighbor remains false.

Wait for timeToOn to elapse so Fire turns ON automatically in Update(). Since Awake() sets timeToOn=random[40,60] and hasDownNeighbor=false (no vertical neighbor), waiting 61s guarantees timeToOnDelta >= timeToOn. Expected behavior: isOn becomes true; shouldTurnOn=false; timeToOnDelta reset; isTurningOff=false; currentHitTime reset; UpdateFireState(true) called to Play mainEffectGo ParticleSystem and notify neighbors and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1).

Test canceling a turn-off sequence: Immediately call Fire.WaterHit(Vector3.zero) once to begin turning off (isTurningOff=true, currentHitInterval=0). Then do NOT call WaterHit again for > maxHitInterval (2s); wait 2.5s. Expected: in Update(), currentHitInterval reaches >= maxHitInterval, so turning off cancels (isTurningOff=false, currentHitTime reset to 0), fire remains ON.

Test full extinguish path: During the next 5.1s, call Fire.WaterHit(Vector3.zero) repeatedly at intervals < maxHitInterval (e.g., every 1.0s) to keep currentHitInterval < 2s. Expected: currentHitTime accumulates to >= totalHitTime (5s), so fire turns OFF (isOn=false, shouldTurnOn=false, timeToOnDelta=0, isTurningOff=false, currentHitTime=0), and UpdateFireState(true) stops ParticleSystem and notifies neighbors and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1).

Simulate neighbors turning OFF: call Fire.NotifyNeighborState(false) twice to decrement onNeighbors below onNeighborsThreshold. Expected: CheckNeighborState() sets shouldTurnOn=false and timeToOnDelta=0. Fire remains OFF and will not auto-reignite without neighbors turning ON again.
